ChangeRequest.name: Preferred abbreviations in Logical data model lead to overlapping attribute names
ChangeRequest.comment:
Some entities of the Logical data model have multiple attributes with the same preferred abbreviation.
The preferred abbreviation is useful as a technical field in any technical software that works with the LDM structures, it matches common constraints on technical naming (clear use of case, no spaces in names, reasonable length of name).
This is a data-modelling concern (it is not related to business logic of regulatory reporting)
We can consider the example of the 'Protection Protection provider assignment' entity.
This entity has 12 attributes, including 2 named:
Protection arrangement reference date
And
Protection provider reference date
Those 2 attributes both have the same preferred abbreviation of DT_RFRNC, see image below
![image](/uploads/25c5311b3fb5be61dd71b83d41d23fb1/image.png)
The preferred abbreviations are used heavily in the BIRD Input layer. Interestingly both attributes are forward engineered to the same field in the BIRD input layer, and so this overlap of naming does not cause problems in that forward engineered model. However I believe the concerns of the forward engineered input layer should not impact the LDM.
I believe this kind of case was already tidied up in many entities, perhaps we missed the ones where it does not cause problems for the input layer.
For example in the entity ‘Other loan Loan debtor assignment’ we have 13 attributes includein 2 named:
Other loan reference date
And
Loan debtor reference date
These have distinct preferred abbreviations of
OTHR_LN_INSTRMNT_RFRNC_DT
And
LN_DBTR_PRTY_RFRNC_DT
Which is much better.
